# C++ Standards â€” HyperI Projects

**Target: C++20** (C++23 where clang supports)

---

## Quick Reference

```bash
cmake -B build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RelWithDebInfo && ninja -C build
./build/unit_tests --gtest_filter='*JSON*'
clang-tidy src/*.cpp -- -std=c++20
clang-format -i src/*.cpp src/*.h
cmake -B build-asan -DSANITIZE=address -GNinja && ninja -C build-asan
cmake -B build-tsan -DSANITIZE=thread -GNinja && ninja -C build-tsan
cmake -B build-ubsan -DSANITIZE=undefined -GNinja && ninja -C build-ubsan
```

## Local-First Development (CRITICAL)

CI takes **30+ min** (ClickHouse fork: 2-3 hrs). Use `ccache`/`sccache`. `ninja -C build` for incremental.

```
Loop: write â†’ ninja -C build â†’ clang-tidy â†’ ./build/unit_tests â†’ commit locally
Push ONLY when complete, or need cross-platform/sanitiser CI, or user asks.
```

| âťŚ Don't | âś… Do | Why |
|----------|-------|-----|
| Push after every commit | Accumulate, push once | 30+ min CI |
| Push to "see if CI passes" | `hyperi-ci check` or local build+test | Faster |
| Push WIP for sanitiser | Run ASan/TSan locally first | Shared queues |

---

## Zero-Copy & Views

| âťŚ Don't | âś… Do | Why |
|----------|-------|-----|
| `void f(const std::string& s)` with substr | `void f(std::string_view s)` | No alloc |
| `void f(std::vector<T> v)` | `void f(std::span<const T> v)` | No copy |
| `const char*` + length | `std::string_view` | Safer |

## Arena Allocation

```cpp
class Arena {
    std::vector<char> buffer;
    size_t offset = 0;
public:
    explicit Arena(size_t cap) : buffer(cap) {}
    char* allocate(size_t sz) { auto* p = buffer.data()+offset; offset+=sz; return p; }
    void reset() { offset = 0; }
};
```

---

## SIMD

- Prefer **Highway** (Google) for portable SIMD, **xsimd** header-only, **simdjson** for JSON
- Raw intrinsics: use aligned loads (`_mm256_load_ps`), handle tail with scalar loop
- `alignas(32)` for buffers; dynamic via `std::aligned_alloc(32, ...)`
- Always handle remainder elements after SIMD loop

---

## Memory Management

- **RAII everywhere** â€” no manual `new`/`delete`
- Wrap C resources (FILE*, etc.) in RAII classes (non-copyable, movable)
- `std::array<char, N>` for small stack allocs

| Ownership | Type |
|-----------|------|
| Unique | `std::unique_ptr<T>` (default) |
| Shared | `std::shared_ptr<T>` |
| Non-owning | `T*` or `T&` |
| Weak ref | `std::weak_ptr<T>` |

---

## Error Handling

- Throw exceptions with context: `throw Exception(ErrorCodes::X, "msg: {}", val);`
- `std::optional` for expected failures (e.g., `tryParseInt`)
- `std::expected` (C++23) for recoverable errors with monadic ops
- Catch at boundaries, not everywhere
- **Never throw in destructors** â€” log instead with `noexcept`

---

## Naming (ClickHouse-Compatible)

| Element | Convention | Example |
|---------|------------|---------|
| Variables/members | `snake_case` | `max_block_size` |
| Functions/methods | `camelCase` | `getName()` |
| Classes/structs | `CamelCase` | `DataTypeString` |
| Constants/macros | `UPPER_SNAKE` | `MAX_BLOCK_SIZE` |
| Template params | `T`, `TValue` | `typename T` |
| Namespaces | lowercase | `DB`, `detail` |

- Variables: lowercase abbreviations (`mysql_connection`)
- Classes: preserve case (`HTTPClient`, `MySQLConnection`)
- Constructor params: underscore suffix (`input_`, `strict_mode_`)

---

## Formatting

- **4 spaces**, braces on own lines (Allman)
- `const` BEFORE type: `const char * pos`
- Pointer/reference: spaces both sides: `char * ptr`, `Block & b`
- Wrap at ~120-140 chars; operators on new line
- Single-statement functions can be one line: `size_t getSize() const { return size; }`
- `.clang-format`: LLVM-based, 120 col, Allman braces, `PointerAlignment: Middle`

---

## Concurrency

| Pattern | Use |
|---------|-----|
| `std::lock_guard` + `std::mutex` | General mutual exclusion |
| `std::shared_mutex` + `shared_lock`/`unique_lock` | Read-heavy workloads |
| `std::atomic` with explicit memory ordering | Counters, flags |
| `std::call_once` | One-time init (NOT manual double-checked locking) |
| Thread pool + `submit()` | Parallel work (never spawn raw threads) |

```cpp
// âś… Atomic counter
std::atomic<uint64_t> count{0};
count.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed);
```

---

## Testing â€” GoogleTest

- Test files: `src/Module/tests/gtest_*.cpp`
- Naming: `TEST_F(ParserTest, ParsesValidJSON)`
- Use `::testing::TestWithParam` for parameterised tests
- `gtest_discover_tests()` in CMake

```cpp
TEST_F(ParserTest, ReturnsErrorForInvalidJSON) {
    auto result = parser->parse("{invalid}");
    EXPECT_FALSE(result.has_value());
}
```

---

## Sanitizers

```cmake
option(SANITIZE "address|thread|undefined|memory" "")
# Sets -fsanitize=X -fno-omit-frame-pointer
```

- Suppress false positives: `__attribute__((no_sanitize("address")))` or suppression files
- `ASAN_OPTIONS=suppressions=asan_suppressions.txt ./program`

---

## Static Analysis

- **clang-tidy**: enable `bugprone-*`, `clang-analyzer-*`, `cppcoreguidelines-*`, `modernize-*`, `performance-*`, `readability-*`; disable `modernize-use-trailing-return-type`, `readability-identifier-naming`, magic-number checks; `WarningsAsErrors: '*'`; cognitive complexity threshold 25
- **Compiler**: `-Wall -Wextra -Werror -Wshadow -Wconversion -Wsign-conversion -Wold-style-cast -Wformat=2 -Wnull-dereference -Wdouble-promotion`

---

## Modern C++ Features

- **Concepts** (C++20): constrain templates â€” `template<Numeric T>`
- **Ranges** (C++20): lazy pipelines with `std::views::filter | transform | take`
- **Structured bindings**: `auto [iter, inserted] = map.insert({k,v});`
- **constexpr**: compile-time computation, lookup tables
- **`std::expected`** (C++23): recoverable errors with `.and_then()`, `.transform()`

---

## Performance Patterns

- **SoA over AoS** â€” contiguous data, cache-friendly
- **Pre-allocate buffers**, reuse with `.clear()` â€” no alloc in hot loops
- **`likely()`/`unlikely()`** branch hints (`__builtin_expect`)
- **`reserve()` + `+=`** for string concat (not `result = result + s`)
- Prefetch: `__builtin_prefetch(ptr, 0, 3)`

---

## Common Pitfalls

| âťŚ Don't | âś… Do |
|----------|-------|
| Return `string_view` to local `string` | Return `std::string` (move semantics) |
| Erase during range-for | `std::erase_if()` or manual iterator |
| Use after `std::move` | Treat moved-from as unspecified |
| Non-virtual destructor on base | `virtual ~Base() = default;` |
| `std::bind` | Lambdas |
| `std::endl` | `'\n'` |
| `NULL` / `0` | `nullptr` |
| C-style cast `(int)x` | `static_cast<int>(x)` |
| Catch by value | `catch (const std::exception& e)` |
| C-style arrays in interfaces | `std::span<T>` |

---

## ClickHouse Fork Specifics (`/projects/ClickHouse`)

| Setting | Value |
|---------|-------|
| C++ Standard | **C++23** (`CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 23`) |
| CMake | â‰Ą3.25 |
| Compiler | **Clang ONLY** (min 19, CI uses 21) |
| Linker | **LLD ONLY** â€” Gold **forbidden**, mold **unsupported** |
| LLVM tools | `llvm-ar`, `llvm-ranlib`, `llvm-objcopy`, `llvm-strip` (version-matched) |
| CI system | **Praktika** (custom Python, NOT GitHub Actions) |
| Build cache | `sccache` |
| `.clang-format` | WebKit-based (140 col, 4-space tabs) |

**Build flags**: `-Xclang -fuse-ctor-homing`, `-falign-functions=64`, `-mbranches-within-32B-boundaries`, ThinLTO for Release (Linux), `_LIBCPP_HARDENING_MODE_EXTENSIVE` in Debug, `ENABLE_CHECK_HEAVY_BUILDS`, `-ffile-prefix-map` for reproducibility.

**CI builds**: DEBUG, RELEASE, ASAN, TSAN, MSAN, UBSAN, TIDY, COVERAGE. Cross: AMD64, ARM64, Darwin, FreeBSD, musl, RISCV64, PPC64LE, S390X. Docker images: `clickhouse/binary-builder`, `clickhouse/fasttest`.

**Contributing**: follow upstream ClickHouse style; HyperI changes in marked sections; keep patches minimal/rebase-friendly; test against ClickHouse CI docker images locally.

---

## Advanced Patterns (ClickHouse Battle-Tested)

### COW Smart Pointers

```cpp
// boost::intrusive_ptr based; Ptr = immutable shared, MutablePtr = exclusive
auto col = Column::create(data);   // MutablePtr
Column::Ptr shared = std::move(col); // Immutable
auto mut = Column::mutate(std::move(shared)); // Clone if refcount>1
```

Use for large objects (columns, blocks) shared across query pipeline.

### PODArray

- No init (unlike `std::vector`), right padding (64B) for SIMD overread, left padding optional
- Non-copyable; `resize()` doesn't call constructors â€” POD only
- Type alias: `PaddedPODArray<T>`

### Arena Allocator

- Hybrid growth: exponential below 128MB, linear above
- SIMD-safe padding (64B), `alignedAlloc()`, ASan integration (`ASAN_POISON/UNPOISON`)
- `ArenaWithFreeLists`: 16 free lists (16Bâ€“512KB), sizes >64KB bypass arena

### Stack-First Allocator

```cpp
template <typename Base, size_t N>
class AllocatorWithStackMemory : private Base {
    alignas(...) char stack_memory[N];
    // Falls back to Base::alloc when N exceeded
};
```

### Multi-Target SIMD Dispatch

`DECLARE_MULTITARGET_CODE(...)` generates Default/SSE42/AVX2/AVX512 namespaces. Runtime dispatch via `CPU::hasAVX512()` etc.

### assert_cast

Debug: `dynamic_cast` + throw on fail. Release: zero-overhead `static_cast`.

### Scope Guards

```cpp
SCOPE_EXIT( cleanup(); );
SCOPE_EXIT_SAFE( cleanup(); );          // catches exceptions, logs
SCOPE_EXIT_MEMORY( cleanup(); );        // blocks memory-limit exceptions
SCOPE_EXIT_MEMORY_SAFE( cleanup(); );   // both
```

### Exception with Stack Capture

`Exception(code, fmt, args...)` captures stack frames at throw site; `markLogged()` prevents duplicate logging.

### Optimised Small memcpy

`memcpySmallAllowReadWriteOverflow15`: 16-byte SSE copies. `memcpySmall`: unrolled switch for 1/2/4/8 bytes.

### Adaptive Prefetch

`PrefetchingHelper`: measures iteration latency, auto-tunes look-ahead distance (4â€“32).

## Project Structure (Greenfield)

```
myproject/
â”śâ”€â”€ .vscode/{settings,launch,tasks,c_cpp_properties}.json
â”śâ”€â”€ cmake/{CompilerWarnings,Sanitizers}.cmake
â”śâ”€â”€ contrib/{googletest,fmt,simdjson}/    # git submodules
â”śâ”€â”€ src/{Common,Core,Module1,...}/        # each with tests/ subdir
â”śâ”€â”€ programs/{server,client}/main.cpp
â”śâ”€â”€ tests/{integration,benchmark}/
â”śâ”€â”€ CMakeLists.txt, CMakePresets.json
â”śâ”€â”€ .clang-format, .clang-tidy
```

- **CMakePresets.json**: presets for debug, release, relwithdebinfo, asan, tsan
- **Module CMake**: `add_library` + `set_project_warnings` + `gtest_discover_tests`
- **VSCode extensions**: `cpptools`, `cmake-tools`, `clangd`, `vscode-lldb`, `clang-format`
- **clangd args**: `--background-index --clang-tidy --completion-style=detailed --header-insertion=iwyu`

---

## AI: Verify Before Suggesting

| âťŚ May not exist | âś… Portable alternative |
|-----------------|----------------------|
| `#include <format>` (C++20, partial) | `fmt` library |
| `#include <expected>` (C++23) | `tl::expected` |
| `#include <generator>` (C++23) | Coroutine libs |
| `std::string::contains()` (C++23) | `str.find(x) != npos` |
| `std::ranges::to<>()` (C++23) | Manual collect |
